case_id: BBBC005_v1_images (synthetic cells with varying focus blur)

pi_items.source: Normalization target confirmed through analysis of BBBC005 images. Value is standard for 8-bit image preprocessing (mid-range of 0-255).
Theoretical target for z-score normalization formula. Formula produces mean=100, std=40 in unclipped floating-point space. However, after required 8-bit clipping, actual std is 13-34, not 40.
Analysis of 50 BBBC005 images normalized with stated formula shows actual means range from 97-98, all within tolerance of ±20 (80-120).

Analysis of 50 BBBC005 images normalized with stated formula and clipped to 8-bit shows actual standard deviations range from 13.13-33.75. This contradicts tolerance of ±15 (range 25-55), as many images fall below 25.
Task description specifies exactly 5 normalized images from sorted BBBC005 w2 files. Files names match expected pattern A01-A05.
Confirmed in task description, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py. Path is created and used consistently throughout.
Confirmed from install_fiji.sh (line 156): 'BBBC005 - Synthetic cells for testing segmentation'. Downloaded from https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC005/BBBC005_v1_images.zip

pi_items.verified_value: 8-bit TIFF (uint8, 696x520 pixels, 2 channels: cell body and nuclei stain)
pi_items.source: Verified by downloading and inspecting BBBC005 files. Images are uint8, contain w1 and w2 variants (wavelengths/channels).

pi_items.verified_value: Formula is mathematically correct z-score normalization. Produces mean=100, std=40 in floating-point. After 8-bit clipping, mean≈97 (acceptable) but std≈13-34 (fails tolerance).
pi_items.source: Manual verification on 5 BBBC005 images. Unclipped: mean=100.00, std=40.00. After clipping: mean=97.29-97.51, std=13.33-16.31.

privileged_info_summary: BBBC005 is a real, publicly available dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection containing 9,600 synthetic cell images (696×520 8-bit TIFF, 2 channels). The task applies z-score normalization with target mean=100 and target std=40. The target_mean of 100 is verified to be achievable (actual: ~97.5, within tolerance ±20). However, the tolerance_std of ±15 (range 25-55) is contradicted by actual results: when applying the stated normalization formula to BBBC005 images with required 8-bit clipping, the resulting standard deviation ranges from 13.1-33.8, with many images below the tolerance minimum of 25. The metadata tolerance_std value is unrealistically tight given the mathematical constraints of clipping normalized values to the 0-255 range.
